briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — aggressive post-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for post-spawn with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Separate the two claws on a Punch Out Craw trailer for a football jig, then hop, drag, and deadstick it along the bottom; in stained water the size and radical claw movement is an ideal combination.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Separate the two claws on a Punch Out Craw trailer for a football jig, then hop, drag, and deadstick it along the bottom; in stained water the size and radical claw movement is an ideal combi
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on point, dock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'Use a 3/4 to 1-ounce football head jig on 12-17 pound fluorocarbon to cover water and locate sweet spots along gravel bars or ridges with isolated stumps. Same slow presentation as a Carolin
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on point, dock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: When fishing a football jig on deep rock, make a long cast, let it hit bottom, and drag it slowly — most bites come when the bait pops off a rock as bass follow it on the drag
A stick bait on a skirtless football head is a productive look for walking down bluff ends

Johnston: Nobody remembers who finishes second in the Classic, so throw big baits and go for the win. Expect big swimbaits, deep-diving crankbaits, Carolina rigs, and football jigs to be the best chances.

KVD: 'Bass don't go extremely deep immediately after spawning. They stop at secondary points, road beds, shallow ledges, main lake points in the 8-10ft range. Many anglers pull out deep divers too soon — a medium-diving crankbait in Sexy Shad matc...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal post-spawn bass are transitioning and aggressive. Males guard fry in 2-6ft while females roam 5-12ft feeding heavily. Target both shallow fry-guarders and roaming post-spawn females near points.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
